title: Uvaria borneensis
taxonomy: Uvaria borneensis is classified in the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This classification places it within the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants known for their complex flowers and often fragrant fruits. The genus Uvaria itself is a genus of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. Uvaria borneensis is a species within this genus, specifically found in Borneo, a large island in Southeast Asia. The Annonaceae family is part of the larger clade Magnoliidae, a group of flowering plants that have been present on Earth for over 100 million years.

intro: Uvaria borneensis, also known as Borneo uvaria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical forests of Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Uvaria borneensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Uvaria borneensis include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including infections and inflammatory diseases.
